Difficulty: Model Selection (Gaussian LMM vs. CLMM)

CongLiu

1 0. Packages and data

need <- c("tidyverse","janitor","lme4","emmeans","broom.mixed","performance","ordinal","knitr","kableExtra")
to_install <- setdiff(need, rownames(installed.packages()))
if(length(to_install)) install.packages(to_install, repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse); library(janitor); library(lme4); library(emmeans); library(broom.mixed)
  library(performance); library(ordinal); library(knitr); library(kableExtra)
})

data_file <- locate_data_file(params$data_path)
df <- readr::read_csv(data_file, show_col_types = FALSE) %>% janitor::clean_names() %>%
  dplyr::rename_with(~"participant", matches("^participant$|^subj|^pid|^ppid", ignore.case=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::rename_with(~"group", matches("^group$|condition|interact|arm", ignore.case=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::rename_with(~"complexity", matches("^complexity$|path_complex|task_complexity|simple|complex", ignore.case=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::rename_with(~"difficulty", matches("^difficulty$|^diff_rating$|difficulty_score|^diff$", ignore.case=TRUE)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    participant = factor(participant),
    group = ifelse(grepl("2D", group, ignore.case=TRUE), "2D+3D", "3D-only"),
    group = factor(group, levels=c("3D-only","2D+3D")),
    complexity = ifelse(grepl("complex", complexity, ignore.case=TRUE), "complex", "simple"),
    complexity = factor(complexity, levels=c("simple","complex")),
    cell = interaction(group, complexity, sep=":"),
    difficulty = as.numeric(difficulty),
    difficulty_ord = ordered(difficulty)  # for CLMM
  )

summary(df$difficulty)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   1.000   2.000   3.000   2.865   4.000   5.000

2 1. Candidate models

# M0: Gaussian LMM, random intercept (primary candidate)
M0 <- lme4::lmer(difficulty ~ group*complexity + (1|participant), data=df, REML=FALSE)

# M1: Gaussian LMM, random intercept + random slope for complexity
M1 <- try(lme4::lmer(difficulty ~ group*complexity + (1 + complexity|participant), data=df, REML=FALSE), silent=TRUE)

# M2: CLMM (ordinal mixed model), random intercept (participant)
M2 <- ordinal::clmm(difficulty_ord ~ group*complexity + (1|participant), data=df, link = "logit", Hess = TRUE)

3 2. Information criteria and singularity checks

get_info_lmer <- function(m, label){
  if(inherits(m,"try-error")) return(tibble(Model=label, Type="Gaussian LMM", logLik=NA, AIC=NA, BIC=NA, Singular=NA))
  tibble(
    Model = label,
    Type = "Gaussian LMM",
    logLik = as.numeric(logLik(m)),
    AIC = AIC(m),
    BIC = BIC(m),
    Singular = performance::check_singularity(m)  # returns TRUE/FALSE
  )
}

# Other functions and code unchanged
get_info_clmm <- function(m, label){
  tibble(
    Model = label,
    Type = "CLMM (ordinal)",
    logLik = as.numeric(logLik(m)),
    AIC = AIC(m),
    BIC = BIC(m),
    Singular = NA
  )
}

# Rebuild the summary table (subsequent code unchanged)
tab <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  get_info_lmer(M0, "M0: Gaussian LMM (RI)"),
  get_info_lmer(M1, "M1: Gaussian LMM (RI+RS)"),
  get_info_clmm(M2, "M2: CLMM (RI)")
) %>% arrange(AIC)

kable(tab, digits=2, caption="Candidate models for Difficulty: logLik/AIC/BIC and singularity") %>% 
  kable_styling(full_width=FALSE)
Candidate models for Difficulty: logLik/AIC/BIC and singularity
Model Type logLik AIC BIC Singular
M0: Gaussian LMM (RI) Gaussian LMM -417.51 847.03 870.73 TRUE
M2: CLMM (RI) CLMM (ordinal) -415.90 847.79 879.40 NA
M1: Gaussian LMM (RI+RS) Gaussian LMM -417.24 850.49 882.09 TRUE 
  performance::check_model(M0)

4 3. Rationale and selection principles (LMM vs. CLMM)

  • Gaussian LMM: treats Difficulty as approximately continuous; given the near-symmetric distribution and acceptable residual/variance diagnostics, this is the primary analysis choice.
  • Random slope (M1): if Singular = TRUE or AIC/BIC do not improve, between-participant variability in the simple–complex contrast is limited; a random-intercept model is sufficient.
  • CLMM (M2): respects the ordinal nature of Likert responses and is used for sensitivity analysis. Information criteria can be inspected, but the key check is whether effect directions and inferential conclusions align with the LMM.

In the subsequent reporting document (03):
- the default primary model is M0 Gaussian LMM (random intercept);
- M2 CLMM is additionally fitted and reported as sensitivity analysis.
